---
title: "Random Variables"
documenttype: Handout
author:
  - name: Saaleh Poovathumkadavil
toc: true
abstract: "A short note on random variables, support, expected value, and how distributions turn outcomes into quantities."

version: [v.1.0]
date: last-modified
execute:
  echo: fenced
  freeze: auto
bibliography: references.bib
bibliographystyle: "springer-humanities-author-date"
format:
  tufte-inspired-html: default
---


# Definition

A random variable assigns a number to each outcome in the sample space. Its
distribution records how probability is spread across those numbers.

This note pairs with [[Bayes Review|statistics/probability/bayes-review]].
For implementation examples in R, see [[Vector Basics|computing/r/vector-basics]].

# Expected Value

The expected value is a probability-weighted average. For a discrete random
variable $X$,

$$
E[X]=\sum_x xP(X=x).
$$
